jpg

Class JpgParser

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.AbstractImageParser<TifMetadata>
      • jpg.JpgParser

  • public class JpgParser
extends AbstractImageParser<TifMetadata>
    A parser for JPG image files designed to extract metadata from the APP segments, handling multi-segment metadata, specifically ICC and XMP data, in addition to the single-segment EXIF data.

    This parser adheres to the EXIF specification (version 2.32, CIPA DC-008-2019), which mandates that all EXIF metadata must be contained within a single APP1 segment. This parser will search for and process the first APP1 segment it finds that contains the Exif identifier.

    For ICC profiles, the parser collects and concatenates all APP2 segments containing the * ICC_PROFILE identifier, adhering to the sequential structural rules defined in the ICC specification. For XMP data, it aggregates standard APP1 segments flagged with the http://ns.adobe.com/xap/1.0/\0 identifier.

    Since:
    22 May 2026
    Version:
    1.9
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class Description
      private static class  JpgParser.JpgSegmentData
      A simple immutable data carrier for the raw byte arrays of the different metadata segments found in a JPEG file, encapsulating the raw EXIF, ICC, and XMP data payloads.

    • Method Detail

      • readMetadata

        public void readMetadata()
        Reads the JPEG file structure to extract and isolate metadata segment streams. This method parses raw underlying APP markers, processes structural EXIF blocks, populates internal metadata records, and reassembles fragmented ICC and XMP structures.
        Specified by:
        readMetadata in class AbstractImageParser<TifMetadata>

      • getMetadata

        public TifMetadata getMetadata()
        Retrieves the extracted metadata from the JPG image file. If the metadata has not been explicitly loaded yet using readMetadata(), it will trigger lazy loading.
        Specified by:
        getMetadata in class AbstractImageParser<TifMetadata>
        Returns:
        a TifMetadata container populated with all successfully extracted directory properties and XMP markers

      • formatDiagnosticString

        public java.lang.String formatDiagnosticString()
                                        throws java.io.IOException
        Generates a human-readable diagnostic string containing metadata details.
        Overrides:
        formatDiagnosticString in class AbstractImageParser<TifMetadata>
        Returns:
        a formatted string suitable for diagnostics, logging, or inspection
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if lower level filesystem attributes are inaccessible

      • stripExifPreamble

        public static byte[] stripExifPreamble(byte[] data)
        Removes the 6-byte Exif\0\0 signature/header required by the JPEG standard before passing the payload to a TIFF parser.
        Parameters:
        data - the raw EXIF chunk payload
        Returns:
        the corrected EXIF data byte array

      • fetchNextSegment

        static JpgSegmentConstants fetchNextSegment(BinaryInput reader)
                                     throws java.io.IOException
        Reads the next JPEG segment marker from the input stream. Note that this method has package-private visibility.

        Markers are identified by a 0xFF byte followed by a non-zero flag. As per the specification, any number of 0xFF fill bytes may precede the actual flag, this method safely discards such padding.

        Parameters:
        reader - the input stream of the JPEG file, positioned at the current read cursor
        Returns:
        a JpgSegmentConstants representing the detected marker, or null if an EOFException occurs, an unmapped flag is found, or excessive padding suggests corruption
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs while reading from the stream

      • readMetadataSegments

        private JpgParser.JpgSegmentData readMetadataSegments(RandomAccessReader reader)
                                               throws java.io.IOException
        Reads all supported metadata segments, including EXIF, ICC and XMP, if present, from the JPEG file stream.
        Parameters:
        reader - the input JPEG stream
        Returns:
        a JpgSegmentData record containing the byte arrays for any found segments
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • reconstructExtendedXmpSegments

        private byte[] reconstructExtendedXmpSegments(byte[] standardXmp,
                                              java.util.List<byte[]> segments)
        Reconstructs split Extended XMP metadata fragments from a JPEG stream by validating them against an anchor GUID and sequentially stitching them back together. *

        References
        For full technical requirements on multi-segment layout design, see the section Extended XMP in JPEG inside the Adobe XMP Specification Part 3: Storage in Files documentation provided by Adobe Systems Incorporated.

        Technical Summary
        The JPEG format limits individual marker segments (like APP1) to 65,535 bytes due to a 16-bit length restriction. Large metadata packages (e.g., depth maps, edit histories) must be sliced across multiple sequential segments. This method implements the rules defined in the Adobe XMP Specification Part 3 (Storage in Files) to locate, validate, order, and merge those segments.

        *

        The Binary Stream Layout
        Each raw byte payload chunk in the segments list must follow this header packet format: 

         +--------------------------+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+------------------------+
 | Bytes 0 - 31 (32 Bytes)  | Bytes 32 - 35 (4 Bytes)       | Bytes 36 - 39 (4 Bytes)       | Bytes 40+ (Variable)   |
 +--------------------------+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+------------------------+
 | MD5 GUID Hex Character   | Total Extended Payload Length | Slice Byte Offset             | Raw Extended XML Data  |
 | String (Matching Anchor) | Big-Endian Unsigned 32-bit    | Big-Endian Unsigned 32-bit    | Fragment Block Payload |
 +--------------------------+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+------------------------+

        Stream Geometry Example
        Below illustrates how chunkTotalLength remains constant across sibling slices to define the final master buffer scale, while chunkOffset increments sequentially to track memory mapping: 

         +---------+--------------------+--------------------+----------------+
 | Segment |  chunkTotalLength  |    chunkOffset     |   dataLength   |
 |         |   (Bytes 32–35)    |   (Bytes 36–39)    | (Payload Size) |
 +---------+--------------------+--------------------+----------------+
 | Chunk 1 | 153,600 (150 KB)   | 0                  | 65,500 bytes   |
 | Chunk 2 | 153,600 (150 KB)   | 65,500             | 65,500 bytes   |
 | Chunk 3 | 153,600 (150 KB)   | 131,000            | 22,600 bytes   |
 +---------+--------------------+--------------------+----------------+
        Parameters:
        standardXmp - the baseline Standard XMP XML block containing the master anchor token link
        segments - a list of raw byte segments extracted from trailing JPEG APP1 blocks, stripped of the namespace prefix string
        Returns:
        a consolidated byte array combining the Standard and full Extended XMP packets, or the original fallback standardXmp array if validation checks fail

      • reconstructIccSegments

        private byte[] reconstructIccSegments(java.util.List<byte[]> segments)
        Reconstruction of a complete ICC metadata block by concatenating multiple ICC profile segments. Segments are ordered by their sequence number as specified in the header.
        Parameters:
        segments - the list of raw ICC segments
        Returns:
        the concatenated byte array, or returns null if no valid segments are available
